methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Vannevar Labs, a Series B-stage defense and security company, is evaluated against a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public firms (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities. The private peer set includes Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, Defense Unicorns, and Heven AeroTech, all at equivalent stage and sector positioning.

Two valuation methods were applied to the company's $1.5B last post-money valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $2.9B. Peer-median analysis examines five same-stage, same-sector comparables with valuations ranging $1.0B to $1.5B, producing a median of $1.0B. This peer cohort anchors at a discount to Vannevar's current mark, suggesting either relative outperformance or valuation reset risk.

Triangulation across both methods supports a range of $850M to $3.3B, with a median of $1.9B. The current $1.5B valuation sits at the lower end of the implied range, slightly above peer median but well below stage-progression expectations. Key uncertainties include the stage-progression multiplier's applicability given defense-sector duration and the peer sample's relative maturity. The wide spread ($850M–$3.3B) reflects methodological divergence; peer comps suggest market discipline, while stage-progression assumes sustained momentum. Resolution hinges on revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and funding-round terms.
